reforms.description: The Government proposed a scheme for supporting stock building, which allows subsidies to cover up to 75 percent of the interest costs arising from loans for stock financing during a specified period.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By subsidizing interest costs for stock financing, the scheme encourages firms to maintain or increase their stock levels, which can support production and economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme was proposed and is in effect for the specified period.
reforms.importance_rationale: This scheme represents a significant intervention to support firms during economic downturns, enhancing liquidity and operational capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The Government proposes a scheme for supporting stock building, according to which subsidies can amount to 75 percent of the interest costs arising from loans for stock financing.
